text.title: AI Trust Disclosure Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-trust-disclosure-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The UI MUST surface model identity (vendor + family + version) at least once per session, accessible within ≤ 2 clicks from any AI-generated output.
rule.rationale: EU AI Act Article 50 (3); Apple HIG 2026; users cannot calibrate trust without knowing which system is speaking.

rule.statement: Every AI-generated output that the system itself classifies as < 90% confidence MUST carry a uncertainty signal (badge / tone / hedge phrase).
rule.rationale: Users systematically over-trust unhedged AI output; explicit uncertainty drops over-trust 30-50% (Anthropic responsible-deployment research).

rule.statement: Factual claims (statements about external entities, numbers, dates, people) MUST be paired with a citable source — link, quote, or 'source unavailable' marker.

rationale.source: Anthropic citations docs; OpenAI safety best practices

rule.rationale: Hallucination acceptance correlates with absence of citation; citation slot forces author to either find a source or declare unavailability.

rule.statement: Users MUST have a one-click opt-out from each AI surface; opt-out persists across sessions and applies system-wide unless re-opted-in.
rule.rationale: Mandatory AI surfaces drive backlash even when the feature is well-built; opt-out preserves trust as users retain control.

rule.statement: Every AI surface MUST offer a per-output error-report channel (thumbs-down + free text); reports MUST be routed to the product team's eval-set growth pipeline.
rule.rationale: Without an error-report channel, regressions are invisible until volume grows; routing reports to eval-set growth closes the loop.
